raw_consumer_statement:
am filing this complaint against Citibank due to a severe and damaging credit reporting error, which directly breaches an official written agreement and resolution provided by their Executive Response Unit. 
On XX/XX/year>, in response to my previous CFPB complaints, Citi 's Executive Response Unit sent me a formal letter ( Citi Reference Number : XXXX ). In this letter, Citi officially confirmed that balance adjustments had been made to bring my account to a XXXX ( {$0.00} ) balance. Furthermore, the letter explicitly assured me : " Our records indicate that no adverse information has been reported to the consumer reporting agencies regarding your Citi Double Cash account. '' Despite these strict written assurances, a review of my TransUnion credit profile as of XX/XX/year>, reveals that Citi is actively reporting XXXX severe derogatory items : XXXX CITICARDS CBNA ( Account ending in XXXX ) : Reported on XX/XX/year>, as a " Collection/Chargeoff '' with a past due balance of {$260.00}. 
XXXX CITI ( Flexible Spending Credit Card ) : Reported on XX/XX/year>, as a " Collection/Chargeoff / Past Due '' with a past due balance of {$630.00}. 
Citi 's automated systems are continuing to report inaccurate, negative " Charge-off '' data to the credit bureaus after their executive team officially closed and cleared the accounts. This ongoing inaccurate reporting constitutes a severe violation of the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ) and is causing immediate, substantial harm to my credit score and financial standing.

Since Citi has failed to internalize their own executive resolution, I am requesting the CFPB 's intervention to compel Citibank to immediately cease this erroneous reporting, submit a Universal Data Form ( UDF ) to delete these negative marks entirely, and accurately reflect a {$0.00} balance with a " Closed at consumer 's request '' status.
